questions.question: Explain why a learning rate that is too large can prevent Gradient Descent from converging.
expectedPoints: Large steps can overshoot the minimum.
This causes the algorithm to oscillate around the optimum.
In severe cases, it can cause divergence, increasing the objective function value.

questions.question: Compare and contrast Batch Gradient Descent, Stochastic Gradient Descent, and Mini-Batch Gradient Descent. Discuss their computational efficiency, convergence behavior, and suitability for large datasets.
expectedPoints: Definition of each variant (amount of data used per update).
Convergence Speed: Batch (slow per epoch), SGD (fast but noisy), Mini-Batch (fast and stable).
Noise in Updates: Batch (none), SGD (high), Mini-Batch (medium).
Suitability: Batch (small datasets), SGD (large/streaming data), Mini-Batch (general purpose, best of both worlds).
